Product Launch Formula PLC 2
Let’s talk about your launch. Let’s talk about the launch that you need to do. You do not
want to show up slowly. Every successful brand, every successful business, every
successful product starts with a successful launch.
There’s a reason you’re watching this video. That’s because either you’ve got a business
and you want to grow that business or you want to start a business.
I’m Jeff Walker and for the last eight years I’ve been training entrepreneurs on how to
launch their businesses and how to launch their products. I’ve trained thousands of
entrepreneurs and they have done hundreds of millions of dollars. So I can say this with
a lot of certainty; there has never been a better time to start a business. There has
never been a time where there’s more opportunity in the world. There has never been a
greater time to be an entrepreneur. These are truly the glory day to launch your
business.
If you look at it, the internet gives you this massive global platform. The tools that are
available to build a business online just weren’t there a few years ago. Frankly, the
training just wasn’t there. Now you have access to amazing training. These are the
glory days to launch your business.
Now, there’s a downside to all of this, there’s a danger. It reminds me of that quote that
goes, “We have met the enemy and he is us.” The deal is that it’s easier than ever for
you to start your business, but it’s also easier for thousands of others. That’s why you
need to launch. That’s why you can’t just depend on hope and hope that your business
does well.
You need to launch. You cannot show up slowly. You cannot start slowly.
It is literally impossible to start slowly and survive. It just doesn’t work that way
because your business withers away. You have cash flow problems, you lose
momentum, you lose energy. You can’t start slowly and survive.
That’s what this is video it all about. It’s about showing you how to launch. The launch
really is beyond just that launch. Where are you going to be three years from now? If
we met three years from now, will you have a thriving business? Will your life be
substantially better than it is now? If we met three years from now, would you be
happy with your progress?
You have to start. You have to launch.
In this video I’m going to be showing you my three main big guns when it comes to
launching. I’ve got more ways to launch, I’ve got six or seven different types of
launches, but I’m going to be showing you the three big guns in this video. These are
the go-to launches that you’re going to use over and over. These launches are really
going to become the framework of your marketing.

-- 2 of 12 --

If you didn’t see my last video on the core concepts and the core elements, look at up
the top and there’s going to be a link to that first video, The Core Elements. Be sure to
check that one out. We’re going to take those elements and we’re going to drop them
into these three big guns, the three big launches.
One of them, the JV launch, that’s how you can start with no list and no product and
basically get paid to build your business and get paid to create your product. Let’s get
started and let’s get to your launch.
I’m not sure what your situation is, but when I first started my business there was only
one word for it, and it was desperation. Back then I was a stay at home dad, I was
home taking care of a couple of babies while my wife was out making a living
supporting the family. We didn’t have much money whatsoever. The family budget at
that point was so tight, our disposable income, meaning the money we had left over
after food and rent, was $400 per year. There was not a lot of money to go around.
I remember one day looking out of the front window, middle of the day, and I see my
wife drives up and I’m like, “What’s going on? Middle of the work day and she’s coming
home?” She comes walking in the door and she was just in tears. She said, “Jeff, I just
need a change. I can’t keep on doing this. I have to have some time with my kids. I’m
getting up in the dark, I’m driving to work, I’m coming home in the dark. Can’t you just
figure out some way to make some money to help support the family to take the
pressure off?”
This was just heartbreaking, because I had nothing going on. I hadn’t made any money
in years. I had just been home taking care of the kids. I had been a square peg in a
round hole in the corporate world, so I left that world behind and I quit to come home
with the kids, my wife was out supporting the family. It was dead end for me.
At that point the only thing I could think of was to try to go back to school, get a
degree, and then maybe get back into the work world. I know this doesn’t sound like a
good plan, because the work world, that corporate world, it was complete failure for me
the first time around. But I thought maybe it would be better the next time around.
I thought, “How can I even get into school? I don’t have anything on my resume to
impress them with.” I came up with this idea to start publishing online. I figured I would
start publishing online, put that on my resume, and I could go to school and then
maybe I’d get a job and I could support the family. So I had this big long term plan.
Looking back, it was a horrible plan, but it was the only one I could come up with
because I just didn’t see any other way out.
So I started publishing online. Back then I had a real interest in the stock market. I
wasn’t a stock broker, I wasn’t a registered investment advisor, I didn’t have any
credentials, any initials after my name, or anything. But I did have a strong interest in
the market and I would study it, I knew a lot about it. So I started publishing about it, I
started publishing a newsletter.
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 3
PLF_Module5v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 3 of 12 --

That first newsletter went out to 19 people. That was basically all my friends, all my
family, everyone I knew who had an email address. This was back in 1996, so not
everyone had email addresses. We certainly didn’t have Facebook back then or any kind
of social media or anything. These were 19 friends and family. I sent that first
newsletter out to those 19 people.
Actually, I’m exaggerating a little bit, because it wasn’t 19 people, it was 19 email
addresses. One of those email addresses was my second email address and one was
my wife’s email address. So it was really 17 people outside of our immediate family. But
19 felt better to me, so I put those names on my distribution list and I sent it out.
I just started publishing gradually. I was actually publishing twice a month. The funny
thing happened was I started to get more subscribers, just through word of mouth. I
put up the most basic website. I couldn’t even afford a domain name back then, so it
was some free web hosting space that came with my email account.
I started publishing and it gradually built up and soon I had hundreds of people on that
list. And then it started to approach 1,000 subscribers. At that point I thought, “Maybe I
could sell these people something.” The problem is that I had no idea how to sell
anything. I had never been a salesman in my life, I hadn’t studied sales, I didn’t know
anything about sales.
I stumbled onto this idea. Frankly, I was scared to ask for the order, I was scared to ask
them to give me any money, because I was giving them this free newsletter. So I just
started romancing them with a bunch of great stuff. I gave them great content after
great content after content. If you think about it, this was the precursor, this is the first
version of that sideways sales letter. I just tried to be super cool to them and give them
great stuff. Then that led up into asking for the sale.
Basically my first product was a paid newsletter about the stock market that I sent out
via email. It was very simple back then. But I romanced them, I gave them all this good
stuff, and then I sent out an email asking them if they wanted to subscribe to my
newsletter. The amazing thing was I actually made some sales. That was the first
Product Launch Formula launch and I did $1,650 in that launch.
I don’t know where you’re at. Money is a funny thing, people look at money in different
ways. For some of you $1,650 would be like, “Why bother?” but for others it would be
amazing. For me back then I was doing backflips over that $1,650, because, like I said,
our disposable income for the year was $400. It was amazing. In fact, at that point
when I did that $1,650 I said, “Wow. If I did this once, I can do it again. And I can do it
again. Who knows, I might even get better at it as I do it again and again.”
That’s when I abandoned that whole idea of going to school and going back to get a
degree and going into the work world. I was done with that because I had this vision of
launch stacking. I didn’t call it that at the time, but I saw this possibility that I might get
better and I might get better.
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 4
PLF_Module5v1.0

-- 4 of 12 --

And the funny thing is that I did get better. The next launch was maybe six months
later. I had built up my list and built up my skills, and the next launch actually did
$6,000. I almost quadrupled my results, $6,000 was amazing. I couldn’t believe it, it
was just over a matter of a few days I made $6,000. Then the next launch did $8,000
and I was like, “This is awesome.”
Then I pulled back and I came up with a new offer and a new product. This is the
launch that brought my wife home. It was a launch that did $34,000. When I did that I
said, “We have to start thinking about having you go to part-time or leave your job,
because we’re on the success train now.”
Then after that one I developed a new product and a new offer. I took everything I had
learned up to then and I really applied this sideways sales letter. Here we’re talking
about the internal launch. The internal launch is the launch that you just do to your list.
You don’t depend on any partners or anything, so you don’t have to go looking for
partners. You just build up your email list. That’s what I did. I started with 19 and I
built that list up. I did internal launch after internal launch using the sideways sales
letter. The three pieces of prelaunch content and then you ask for the order.
That next launch was the one that sort of rocked the marketing world after word of it
got out, because that one did $106,000 in a single week, in seven days. That became
known sort of famously in the marketing world as six figures in seven days.
Now, this didn’t happen overnight. The first one was $1,650 was within a few months.
The $34,000 was within a year and a half of getting started. The $106,000 was four
years later, so it didn’t happen overnight. Then again, I didn’t have anyone to train me
on this stuff, I was inventing everything.
That list of 19 subscribers eventually built up to 80,000 subscribers. You do that just by
adding 30 subscribers a day. That’s what I did, I added 30 subscribers a day, that adds
up to almost 1,000 subscribers a month, that’s 12,000 a year. This is a real business I
was building and I was doing that launch stacking. Every launch was getting better and
better.
Launch stacking is this idea of you build your list, you build your momentum, you build
your skills, you build your offers, you build your knowledge of the market, and your
launches get better and better.
The amazing thing is that with the humble beginnings I had, 19 subscribers when I
started out, not having a clue about how to start a business, not having sold anything in
my life or having any sales experience whatsoever, not having anyone to train me or
show me anything because this was back in the internet dark ages, I was able to sell a
million dollars worth of product. That didn’t happen overnight, it actually took me six
years to get there. Yes, I actually sold a million dollars. This was my product that I
created, a million dollars, and it took six years to get there.

-- 5 of 12 --

Again, that might sound like a long time for me. Where I was at with $400 of
disposable income it was unbelievable success, I could not believe it. My wife was able
to retire, she was home with the kids, it was amazing.
I actually brought on a partner in that business. One day he decided to go a different
way and the partnership broke up, so it was time for me to start a new business.
Frankly, I was ready to leave the stock market behind, it was getting to be a grind
publishing all the time. So I decided to start this new business and this time I decided
to use a seed launch.
That first launch I told you about, that internal launch, I had been using internal
launches in this business, but the second time around I wanted a seed launch because I
wanted to be able to create a product basically in partnership with my clients and I
wanted to get paid up front. This time I went with the seed launch.
The seed launch is great if you have no list and no product. You want to think about it
like you need to have a small list, 50 to 100 people would be great. These days it’s easy
to get that in social media. If you just start publishing about something on Facebook, a
couple times a week publish about whatever topic you want to start your business in,
you can attract followers very quickly. Go out and take part in the social media
discussions. It’s very easy to build those. Go crazy, get 200 or 300 people on the list.
That would be even better. I’ve seen people do this starting with 30 or 40 people.
This is how the seed launch looks. Think of these dots as maybe 10 people per dot or
so. I don’t know how many dots I have there, but let’s just say it’s 12 of them and we
have 120 people on the list. The thing about really small lists like this is they tend to be
very responsive. Small lists are extremely responsive because of this concept of
recency, the other one is that they know you. They really feel like they know you, you
feel like you know them, and you probably have interactions with them. So small lists
can be very responsive.
You put together a very simple sideways sales letter. Basically just a couple pieces of
prelaunch content, as opposed to over here with the internal launch where you really
do the classic three pieces of prelaunch content then the sales letter, here you could do
one or two pieces of prelaunch.
One of the things I want to mention is the seed launch is great if you’re just starting
out. It’s absolutely great for that. It’s also great if you have a current business, but you
have a new offer you want to come out with, you want help creating that product, you
want to test out an offer. I still use seed launches to this day. I did one just a few
months ago, it was so much fun and I loved doing it.
You put out a couple of small pieces of prelaunch content and then the way you make
your offer is that you’re actually going to deliver it via a series of calls. Let’s say you
were going to teach guitar.
The message in the prelaunch could be, “People have been asking me, I’ve been
publishing on my blog, I’ve been publishing on Facebook with tips about how to learn
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 6
PLF_Module5v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 6 of 12 --

guitar, people keep on asking me if I would just go ahead and teach it. So what I’ve
decided to is put together a series of teleseminars,” – or webinars, you can pick
whichever one you want.
You put together a series of teleseminars and then you deliver your content over three
teleseminars. You could do five if you have a more in-depth topic and you need five
calls to cover it, but you could do just three calls. What you do is make this offer,
generally what you’re looking for is it would be ideal to get 20 or 30 people in your
program. I’ll explain why.
After they go through your mini sideways sales letter and then buy from you, the first
thing you do is send them to a survey. In the survey you’re asking them what they need
to know about the topic. It could be just like, “I’m really excited about this program. I’m
getting ready to start teaching it. I just need to make sure that I cover this topic
completely. Could you do me a favor and just tell me if you’re going to sit down with me
for lunch or for coffee, what would be the top two questions you would ask me?” You
send your people through that survey, those questions tell you what you need to cover
in your product.
After that first survey you could just take a look at it, take all the questions that
everyone sent in and group them into three main topics. Those three topics are your
calls. Then you do your first call and you’re basically getting on there and answering the
questions people have asked you.
Then after that call you run them through another survey that basically says, “That was
a great call. I loved teaching that, loved being on the call with you. Great interaction. I
just have a couple of questions for you. Is there anything that isn’t 100% clear about
what I covered in that last call?” That’s the first question. The second question is, “Our
next call is going to be about ______. What are your topic two questions about that?”
Then that tells you what you need to cover in that next call. Then you just keep on
doing that; survey, call, survey, call, for as many calls as you want to do.
One of the things that will make all the difference for your business is that you always
want to over deliver. This is something I absolutely obsess about. It’s really easy in the
seed launch to over deliver by just doing a bonus call that you don’t announce up front.
You don’t announce it until you’re somewhere between the first and the second call.
You just say, “Hey, by the way, I told you that you’re going to get three calls, but we’re
actually going to have bonus call. The bonus call is going to be the best call of them all
and you’re going to love it.”
Then after your final regular call, the call you promised, then you send them to another
survey and say, “I just want to know if there’s any questions whatsoever. We’re going to
have a wide open Q&A on this next call,” and then you send them into the bonus call.
The whole entire process there’s so much interaction, because there are so many
surveys, and most of the calls you can actually take live questions. I love taking live
questions on my call. Because you’re doing that all the way through, you are going to
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 7
PLF_Module5v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 7 of 12 --

create a great product. A lot of people worry about creating products and they worry
that their product isn’t going to be good enough or that it’s not going to be perfect.
This product is tuned to the market, because every step of the way you’re asking
people what they need to know. It just creates the best product you ever could. You
end up with very happy clients. You generally end up with great testimonials. You
usually end up with great case studies.
And then because you’re recording these calls all the way through, you end up with the
recordings. If you did three calls and they each lasted an hour to 90 minutes, you’re
going to end up with four or five hours of audio. You can take that and get it
transcribed. Those four or five hours of audio are probably going to be 60 – 80 – maybe
even 90 pages of content. Now you have an audio product, you have a book, and
you’ve learned how to teach it, you’ve learned how to create a great product. That’s
why this is just such an amazing and simple process.
Frankly, the technical stuff is super simple. You’re using what we call a bridgeline to do
those teleseminars. You can get a free one at FreeConferenceCall.com. The technology
is basically you have to dial into a number and everyone dials into that number, so then
they can hear you and then you talk into the phone and you teach.
Or you can do it via webinar. That’s a little bit more complicated, but not much more
complicated. This is simple stuff.
In any case, the second business, this sort of Jeff 2.0 where I was reinventing myself, I
did the seed launch. I did it slightly different, I was actually on stage giving a
presentation for this new product in this new market. I basically gave a 75 minute
presentation that was the best presentation I could give in the world, and then I made
an offer, “I’m going to have a deeper training on this if you want to be part of it. It’s
going to be a series of five teleseminars. Here it is, buy it from me.” That started as a
75 minute presentation where I really went through it in depth, that was sort of my
prelaunch content, and then I made the offer.
The thing is that because I’m not a salesman, because I’m not a pitchman, this was the
first time I ever tried to sell something from stage like that. There’s a real art, and I
didn’t know the art. So I only sold six copies.
The good news is I charged a lot of money for it. Remember, this is a new business, it
had been a few months since my old business went away, so I was really interested in
making some money, so I did charge a lot of money for it. I actually charged $3,000. So
that six sales was $18,000.
Bad news, six sales when I’m speaking to a room with nearly 300 people in that room.
If you don’t have a frame of reference, selling six sales to 300 people is really pretty
lame, it’s lousy. That’s the bad part. The good part was I made $18,000. Compared to
my start in the first business, Jeff 2.0, $18,000 – woohooo!
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 8
PLF_Module5v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 8 of 12 --

Then what I did was I promised them five calls and because I completely obsess about
over delivery, I think it was eight or nine calls in total that I did. But really this was a
new market and all those calls helped me fine tune my new product, it helped me learn
how to teach, learn how to create that product. That’s the key about the seed launch is
that you get started right away, you get paid right away, you build some momentum
right away, but you end up creating a fantastic product and you create it very easily
because all you’re doing is just talking on the phone and answering questions. It’s
simple.
I did that whole series of eight or nine teleseminars, people loved it. I got great
feedback, I actually got a bunch of case studies and testimonials. Then I did what they
call the JV launch.
I’ve got these numbered one, two, and three. The typical way you’ll do this is you’ll
start off with a seed launch, then you’ll build up to an internal launch, and then you’ll
go to a JV launch. I did it a little backwards. Since I was inventing this in the first place,
that first business these two didn’t even exist, this was the only thing that existed and I
invented it. So this was the first one I invented, so I did the internal launch first.
This second business I skipped, I did a seed launch and then I went straight into a JV
launch, because I had no list whatsoever in this market, but I did know some JV
partners. JV stands for joint venture. Think of it like an affiliate. Someone else who has
a list of subscribers and they will mail their list and tell them about your product. In
exchange, if you make sales to the people they send into your launch then you will give
them a commission.
This is what a JV launch looks like. It’s basically the same sideways sales letter as you
did up there, but here you’re having your joint venture partners who already have the
lists and they’re mailing into your launch. Up here you have a sideways sales letter,
three pieces of prelaunch content – three pieces of prelaunch content down here. Often
it could be the same piece of prelaunch content.
In other words, often you could do an internal launch, put together your sideways sales
letter, you prelaunch content, and then after that internal launch you could do a JV
launch after you’ve figured out your numbers, your metrics, your offer, your systems,
then you can go recruit joint venture partners and do the sideways sales letter down
here. The big difference is you’re just getting these partners who have lists to mail into
your sideways sales letter.
The great thing about a JV launch is they can be so highly leveraged. Think about up
here, even at the end when I had 80,000 subscribers, I had 80,000 but what if you got
three or four of five joint venture partners and each one of them had 50,000
subscribers. You can see how these can be highly leveraged and take your results to a
completely different level. That’s what joint venture launches do.
I was coming out of here, I knew I had a great product that I had created with the
seed launch. Again, I do obsess about great product, so what I did was I took what I
learned in this process and I re-recorded everything and I created books, CDs, DVDs,
Copyright © All Rights Reserved Internet Alchemy, Inc. 2014 -2015 	Page ! 9
PLF_Module5v1.0
GROUPINSIDERS.COM EXCLUSIVE

-- 9 of 12 --

and on and on. But I took what I learned in this process about the teaching and about
the selling, and about what could really make a difference in people’s lives with this
product, and then I went out and recruited some joint venture partners and did a joint
venture launch.
I got a lot of great support from these joint venture partners. Again, I had started with
six sales and $18,000, but with the JV launch I did $600,000. Not a bad way to start
this business. First business was $1,650 and I was really happy with that, but $600,000
– holy cow.
The thing with that is when you have joint venture partners you end up paying them
commissions. So in this case I was paying 50% commissions, so I paid my partners
$300,000 in commissions, that left me with $300,000. I had some additional costs, but
still I made a ton of money.
Of course, when you do one of these launches the joint venture partners are going out
talking about how great you are and that gives you great positioning in the market. All
of a sudden people see you as an expert or a guru. And of course the sales didn’t stop
at $600,000. I kept on making sales. In this business, this is sort of the Jeff 2.0, it took
six years to hit a million dollars in that first business, this time it took me six months to
get to a million dollars in sales. That was awesome.
This business was actually my current business. This was my first big launch for Product
Launch Formula. That put me in the game, I got all these new clients, I got all these
great testimonials, all these great case studies.
One of these things when you over deliver is that you build up this massive goodwill in
the market and all of a sudden people are saying great things about you. You can’t ever
underestimate how powerful that is. It is just incredibly powerful.
Then a couple of years later I came out with the 2.0 version of Product Launch
Formula. That 2.0 version, huge massive upgrade, and I had been doing so well in the
market and making my partners so much money that I was able to get a lot more
support that second time around. This time I hit $1,100,000 in the very first hour.
The prelaunch sent people into that sales letter and I did $1,100,000 in that first hour.
Sales didn’t stop after that first hour, we went on to do almost $4,000,000 in sales in 34
hours. Pretty amazing.
Again, it hasn’t stopped there. We’ve now sold $20,000,000 worth of Product Launch
Formula.
Here we are over here, a million dollars took six years the first time, six months the
second time, and 60 minutes the third time. That’s launch stacking. It’s like starting off
here with 19 subscribers, building up to $1,650, and continually getting better – 6,000 –
8,000 – 34,000 - $106,000. You’re building up your momentum, you’re building up your
list, you’re building up your positioning, you’re building up your knowledge of how to
sell, you just get better every launch.

-- 10 of 12 --

Then I had built up a lot of skills, I had learned how to do it, so then I came out with
the joint venture launch $20,000,000 in sales later. It’s been a crazy ride. I can hardly
believe it. If I hadn’t lived through it, I don’t think I would believe it. But that is the very
essence of launch stacking.
Three types of launches; the seed launch, the internal launch, the JV launch. Typically
when you’re starting out you’ll start out with the seed launch, then go to an internal
launch, and go to a JV launch. But enormously flexible, you saw I sort of jumped
around. I started out with the internal launch. The second time around I went straight
from seed launch to JV launch. That is the potential, that’s what launch stacking can do
for you.
So let’s talk about you. That’s enough talking about me. The only reason I shared my
journey is so that you could see what was possible. The thing is I get a lot more excited
talking about my students and my clients, and I’ve seen many of them make crazy
journeys as well where they just started from scratch and have built serious businesses.
If you’re watching this, you probably fall into one of three categories.
First, you’ve got an existing business and you’re looking to build that.
Second, you might not have an online business, you might be some type of practitioner,
a health practitioner or you’re helping people with their money or their wellness, or with
a hobby or with a sport, and maybe you’re working with people one-on-one and it’s
time for you to start leveraging your time, it’s time for you to start getting products out
there.
The third category is maybe you’re absolutely starting from scratch, you don’t have a
list, you don’t have a product, you don’t have a business, you don’t even have an idea
for a business and you don’t know where to start.
The thing is those three launches can fit every one of those categories.
If you’ve got that great business already, then you are just one internal launch away
from having the biggest week of your business history. It’s super easy. Or if you have a
new product you want to roll out and you’re not so sure about it, you can do a seed
launch. An internal launch, or a seed launch, or possibly maybe you have some
potential JV partners and you could do a JV launch, that’s where you really leverage up.
If you’re in that second category and you’re a practitioner of some type, maybe you
have clients, those clients would love to buy something from you but you don’t have a
product yet, the seed launch is perfect. That’s how you create a product that you know
the market wants, that’s how you know you’re creating a great product. Do that seed
launch first. Easy money, easy way to create that product, and then use that product
and do a full out internal launch.
If you’re absolutely just starting from scratch, then it’s all about the seed launch for
you. This is how you create a product as easily as possible, as quickly as possible, and
you don’t have to spend a ton of time. You don’t want to wait around and build up a

huge list, you don’t want to wait around and look for affiliates or joint venture partners,
you just get started really quickly with that seed launch.
I can’t wait to hear your story three months or six months from now of how you were
able to start with that seed launch.
